calendar.matters:
Petition Regarding Title To Or Possession Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff, for and with Petitioner Darren Badong
The Court stated that the issue regarded property taken out of trust for a refinancing, and determination of intent to return it into the trust. Mr. Shustoff stated that a declaration had been filed on June 5, 2015 to further address this questioon following the previous hearing. The Court replied that there was still no evidence of the trustor's intent regarding the property returning into the trust after it was removed from the trust to accomplish the refinancing. Mr. Shustoff contended that the supplemental declaration showed that Petitioner always knew his parents intended to get the property. The Court conceded that such may be true, but it was still unclear if that was intended to be by the trust or under the will. The matter was taken under submission.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

Petition Regarding Title To Or Possession Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Continued To Aug-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Regarding Title To Or Possession Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Continued To Jul-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Regarding Title To Or Possession Of Trust Property
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Continued To Jul-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for (Trustee & Petitioner) Darren Badong.

The Court stated that the property was never titled to the trust but was listed as trust property, that it was part of the refinancing transaction but never put back into the trust, that the trustee does not say to the extent to which he was involved in his parents affairs or how he has knowledge of what occurred. Mr. Shustoff stated that Mr. Badong was not involved in the refinancing or in creating the trust, that they assume the parents thought the property was in the trust, that there have been no objections from the heirs and that nothing leads us to believe that he did not intend for this property to be in the trust. The Court stated that if someone involved in the transaction could testify it would bolster the case. The Petition regarding title to or possession of trust property was continued to July 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for supplemental evidence.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
